curation_notes: Locations derived from the patch diff. The primary vulnerability is in src/sequelize.js in the query method, which called Utils.format and Utils.formatNamedParameters to perform naive string-based replacement of positional (?) and named (:name) parameters. These utility functions in src/utils.js did not parse SQL structure (strings, comments, column identifiers), so user values containing replacement markers could trigger a second substitution pass. The patch replaced these calls with a new injectReplacements function in src/utils/sql.ts that properly parses SQL token boundaries before substituting. Five non-test source files were changed (including type definitions and deprecation comments), making this a noisy patch.
